name: Acute tadalafil increased muscle-damage markers without antioxidant benefit in fit men
statement: In 12 healthy fit men, a single tadalafil 20 mg dose increased CK and LDH 24 hours after consumption without improving antioxidant status, oxidative target molecules, or polyamine levels.
population_or_context: Healthy men with high cardiorespiratory fitness.
time_horizon: Baseline to 24 hours after a single tadalafil dose.
quantitative_note: VO2max range was 40.1-56.0 mL/kg/min; CK and LDH increased at 24 hours, while redox homeostasis, antioxidant enzyme activities, oxidation target molecules, and polyamines were unchanged.

source_locator.quote_or_paraphrase_note: Abstract reports sample, single-dose exposure, biomarkers, and conclusion.

population_profile: Healthy fit men; relevant to active healthy users but not proof of clinical harm or benefit.
endpoint_measure: CK, LDH, TAC, GSH/GSSG, free thiols, antioxidant enzymes, TBARs, protein carbonyls, spermine, spermidine.
effect_estimate: CK and LDH increased 24 hours after tadalafil; antioxidant/redox/polyamine markers were not improved.
safety_findings: Potential muscle-damage biomarker caution signal after acute tadalafil in fit men.
directness_to_target_population: Moderate for fit healthy men, low for clinical injury or performance.
confounding_and_bias_limits: Small uncontrolled pre-post design, biomarkers only, no exercise performance measure.
class_transfer_limits: Direct tadalafil evidence, but uncontrolled biomarker data cannot establish ergogenic or recovery claims.
